title: mode-API Document-PaddlePaddle Deep Learning Platform
description: Used to find values and indices of the modes at the optional axis.

parameters.name: Parameters
parameters.description: x(Tensor) – Tensor, an input N-D Tensor with type float32, float64, int32, int64.axis(int,optional) – Axis to compute indices along. The effective range is [-R, R), where R is x.ndim. when axis < 0, it works the same way as axis + R. Default is -1.keepdim(bool,optional) – Whether to keep the given axis in output. If it is True, the dimensions will be same as input x and with size one in the axis. Otherwise the output dimensions is one fewer than x since the axis is squeezed. Default is False.name(str|None,optional) – For details, please refer toapi_guide_Name. Generally, no setting is required. Default: None.

parameters.name: Returns
parameters.description: tuple (Tensor), return the values and indices. The value data type is the same as the inputx. The indices data type is int64.

If it is True, the dimensions will be same as input x and with size one in the axis. Otherwise the output dimensions is one fewer than x since the axis is squeezed. The indices data type is int64.Examples>>>importpaddle>>>tensor=paddle.to_tensor([[[1,2,2],[2,3,3]],[[0,5,5],[9,9,0]]],dtype=paddle.float32)>>>res=paddle.mode(tensor,2)>>>print(res)(Tensor(shape=[2, 2], dtype=float32, place=Place(cpu), stop_gradient=True,[[2., 3.],[5., 9.]]), Tensor(shape=[2, 2], dtype=int64, place=Place(cpu), stop_gradient=True,[[2, 2],[2, 1]]))
raw_html:
mode

paddle. mode ( x: Tensor, axis: int = -1, keepdim: bool = False, name: str | None = None ) → tuple[Tensor, Tensor] [source]

Used to find values and indices of the modes at the optional axis.

Parameters

  • x (Tensor) – Tensor, an input N-D Tensor with type float32, float64, int32, int64.

  • axis (int, optional) – Axis to compute indices along. The effective range is [-R, R), where R is x.ndim. when axis < 0, it works the same way as axis + R. Default is -1.

  • keepdim (bool, optional) – Whether to keep the given axis in output. If it is True, the dimensions will be same as input x and with size one in the axis. Otherwise the output dimensions is one fewer than x since the axis is squeezed. Default is False.

  • name (str|None, optional) – For details, please refer to api_guide_Name. Generally, no setting is required. Default: None.

Returns

tuple (Tensor), return the values and indices. The value data type is the same as the input x. The indices data type is int64.

Examples

>>> import paddle

>>> tensor = paddle.to_tensor([[[1,2,2],[2,3,3]],[[0,5,5],[9,9,0]]], dtype=paddle.float32)
>>> res = paddle.mode(tensor, 2)
>>> print(res)
(Tensor(shape=[2, 2], dtype=float32, place=Place(cpu), stop_gradient=True,
[[2., 3.],
 [5., 9.]]), Tensor(shape=[2, 2], dtype=int64, place=Place(cpu), stop_gradient=True,
[[2, 2],
 [2, 1]]))
